relevant_files.role: Current security patterns in JSON format

conventions.commits: Conventional commits with GPG signing and DCO sign-off
conventions.testing: Unit tests in same file as implementation, integration tests in tests/ directory
conventions.linting: clippy with pedantic settings, rustfmt for formatting
conventions.error_handling: anyhow for application errors, thiserror for library errors

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Documents the current limitation that tree-sitter would address

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: The target issue for this research

constraints: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing patterns
Should not significantly increase scan time
Must support multiple programming languages (Rust, Python, JavaScript, etc.)
test_coverage: Comprehensive unit and integration tests for security scanner, including tests for various vulnerability types and edge cases. Current limitation test documents multi-line detection issue.

library_findings.notes: Core tree-sitter parsing library with Rust bindings
Rust language grammar for AST parsing
Python language grammar for AST parsing
JavaScript/TypeScript language grammar for AST parsing

approaches.name: Hybrid AST+Regex Engine
approaches.description: Enhance current PatternEngine with tree-sitter AST analysis while keeping regex patterns as fallback. Create AST-based patterns alongside regex patterns, with preference given to AST patterns when available.
pros: Can leverage both AST precision and regex simplicity
cons: Need to maintain two pattern systems
Potential performance overhead from dual analysis

approaches.name: Pure AST Engine with Migration
approaches.description: Replace PatternEngine entirely with tree-sitter based engine. Create new ASTPattern type and migrate existing patterns to tree-sitter queries. Maintain regex engine temporarily for unsupported languages.
cons: Breaking change requiring major version bump
Steeper learning curve for pattern authors

approaches.name: AST Query Layer
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter as an optional layer that runs after regex scanning. Use tree-sitter queries to detect multi-line patterns that regex misses. Keep existing regex patterns for single-line cases.
pros: Can be enabled/disabled via feature flag
cons: Still limited by regex patterns for single-line cases

recommendation: Hybrid AST+Regex Engine approach is recommended because it provides the best balance between addressing the multi-line detection limitation while maintaining backward compatibility. This approach allows for gradual migration to AST-based patterns without breaking existing functionality, and provides a clear path for future enhancements. The complexity is justified by the long-term benefits of AST-based analysis.
